subject: FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING
preamble: This syllabus covers basic accounting principles, double entry systems, preparation of financial statements, and specialized accounting systems to assess candidates' comprehension and application of accounting concepts across various business entities.
objectives: Understanding of accounting principles and the role of accounting in recording business transactions.
Appreciation and application of the rules and functions of Accounting as they apply to organization.
Foundation for further studies in Accounting.

examination_scheme.summary: There will be two papers, Paper 1 and Paper 2, both of which must be taken.

papers.title: Multiple Choice
papers.details: Fifty multiple choice questions covering the entire syllabus. All questions should be answered.

papers.title: Theory and Practice
papers.details: Consists of two sections: Section A (Theory of Financial Accounting) with 4 questions (answer 2 for 30 marks) and Section B (Financial Accounting Practice) with 5 questions (answer 3 for 45 marks).

topics.title: Introduction to Financial Accounting
details: 1.1 History, nature and functions of Accounting.

topics.title: The Accounting Equation and Double Entry Principles
details: 2.2 Purpose and functions of source documents.
2.5 Cash Book: analytical cash book, including petty cash book.
2.8 Correction of errors and Suspense Account.

topics.title: The Final Accounts of a Sole Trader/proprietorship
details: 4.1 Trading, profit and loss accounts/Income statement.

topics.title: Provisions and Reserves
details: 5.1 Provision for doubtful debts/Allowance for doubtful debts.
5.3 Depreciation - concepts, reasons for recording and methods: (i) straight line; (ii) reducing balance; (iii) sum of the years digits; (iv) revaluation.

topics.title: Manufacturing Accounts
details: 6.3 Preparation of final Accounts of Manufacturing concern.

topics.title: Control Accounts and Self-balancing Ledgers
details: 7.1 Meaning and uses of control accounts.
7.2 Types: (i) sales ledger control, (ii) purchases ledger control.

topics.title: Single Entry and Incomplete Records
details: 8.2 Computation of profit or loss from opening and closing balance sheets.
8.3 Conversion of single entry to double entry.
8.4 Preparation of final accounts from a set of incomplete records.

topics.title: Accounts of Not-for-Profit Making Organizations
details: 9.7 Profit or loss from income generating activities.

topics.title: Partnership Accounts
details: 10.4 Partners capital account and balance sheet.
10.6 Treatment of goodwill and revaluation of assets.
10.7 Dissolution of partnership (Questions will not be set on Garner V. Murray and piecemeal realization).

topics.title: Company Accounts
details: 11.1 Nature and formation of a company.
11.4 Loan capital, debentures/loan notes and mortgages.
11.5 Final accounts of company for internal use only.
11.6 Interpretation of accounts using simple ratios.
11.8 Statement of Cash Flow (using direct and indirect methods). Note: Separate questions may be set to meet statutory requirements of individual countries.

topics.title: Departmental and Branch Accounts
details: 12.2 Differences between a department and branch.
12.4 Preparation of Branch Account excluding foreign branches.

topics.title: Public Sector Accounting
details: 13.1 Meaning and difference between Public Sector and Private Sector Accounts.

topics.title: Accounting for Value Added Tax (Ghana only)
Information Technology in Accounting (Ghana only)
details: 15.1 Manual and computerized Accounting Processing Systems.
15.4 Merits and demerits of manual and computerized accounting processing systems.

topics.title: Miscellaneous Accounts (Nigeria, The Gambia, Sierra Leone and Liberia only)
details: 16.1 Meaning, introduction, terminologies and preparation of simple: (i) Joint Venture Accounts, (ii) Consignment Accounts, (iii) Contract Accounts, (iv) Hire Purchase Accounts.

topics.title: Financial System (Nigeria, The Gambia, Sierra Leone and Liberia only)
details: 17.2 Meaning, functions and features of: (i) money market; (ii) capital market; (iii) insurance market.
17.3 Methods of raising funds from the capital market: (i) offer for sale; (ii) offer for subscription; (iii) rights issue; (iv) private placement.
17.4 Requirements for accessing the capital market.
17.5 Benefits of capital market to: (i) investors; (ii) government; (iii) economy; (iv) individual company.
17.6 Types, features and reasons for regulation.

reading_list: Business Accounting Volume 1 by Frank Wood (West African Edition).
Business Accounting Volume 2 by Frank Wood.
Accounting and Finance by A. H. Millchamp.
Foundation Accounting by J. D. Magee.
Basic Accounting by S. C. Malhorta, P. K. Botchwey, and P. A. Omunya.
Accounting for Senior Secondary School by Amankwah.
Accounting in Business by R. J. Bull.
Company Accounts by J. N. Amorin.
Principles of Accounting by K. B. Appiah Mensah.
